description: Add a scope-relative JsonPathExpression helper for plan compilation. Acceptance: given DbTableModel.JsonScope (absolute) and a DbColumnModel.SourceJsonPath (absolute), produce a scope-relative JsonPathExpression by stripping the scope's prefix segments. If the column path exactly equals the scope path, return canonical value-at-scope "$" (zero segments). Validate the scope is a true prefix (or equal) and that no [*] segments remain after stripping; fail fast otherwise.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for: exact scope match -> "$"; scope-not-prefix failure; leftover [*] failure

description: Compile deterministic stored-column bindings for a single table (no SQL yet). Acceptance: for a DbTableModel, enumerate stored/writable columns (ColumnStorage.Stored only) in deterministic order, derive WriteColumnBinding.ParameterName using PlanNamingConventions, and derive WriteValueSource using: DocumentId, ParentKeyPart(i), Ordinal, Scalar(scope-relative path + scalar type), DocumentReference(binding index), DescriptorReference(descriptor resource + scope-relative path), and Precomputed (only as a placeholder for storage-only columns until key-unification compilation is added). UnifiedAlias columns must be excluded from bindings (fail fast if encountered as writable).
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that assert: binding order is deterministic; parameter names are stable; scalar paths are compiled as scope-relative (including "$" value-at-scope)

description: Emit InsertSql for a table from its compiled bindings. Acceptance: emitted InsertSql uses SimpleInsertSqlEmitter with ordered columns matching TableWritePlan.ColumnBindings order and ordered parameters matching WriteColumnBinding.ParameterName order. SQL must be canonicalized (\n only, stable indentation, dialect quoting) and byte-for-byte stable across runs.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that compile the same model twice (and with permuted input ordering) and assert identical InsertSql for both pgsql and mssql

description: Emit UpdateSql for 1:1 tables. Acceptance: emit UpdateSql for any table whose key contains no ColumnKind.Ordinal, but only when at least one stored non-key column exists to set. UpdateSql must use key columns in key order and must reuse the same parameter names already assigned in ColumnBindings (no re-derivation). SQL must be canonicalized and stable for both dialects.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering: (a) UpdateSql present for root and root-scope _ext 1:1 tables with non-key columns, (b) UpdateSql omitted when only key columns exist

description: Emit DeleteByParentSql for replace semantics. Acceptance: for every non-root table plan (any table other than resourceModel.Root), emit DeleteByParentSql with WHERE predicates on exactly the table key columns excluding the table's own ColumnKind.Ordinal (if present), in key order. Parameter names in DeleteByParentSql must reuse the same bare parameter names as the corresponding key columns' WriteColumnBinding.ParameterName. Root-scope _ext 1:1 tables must also emit DeleteByParentSql that deletes by DocumentId (so runtime can remove the row when $._ext.{p} is absent). SQL must be canonicalized and stable for both dialects.
steps-to-verify: Add/adjust unit tests covering delete predicates for: (a) a direct child table, (b) a nested child table, and (c) a root-scope _ext 1:1 table

description: Compute BulkInsertBatching for every compiled TableWritePlan. Acceptance: BulkInsertBatchingInfo is derived via PlanWriteBatchingConventions from ColumnBindings.Count (parameters per row) and stored on each TableWritePlan. Batch size derivation must fail fast when a table's parameter width exceeds the dialect limit. Add coverage for both pgsql and mssql.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests validating batching calculations for representative small and large column counts

description: Compile ResourceWritePlan.TablePlansInDependencyOrder for multi-table resources. Acceptance: for each ResourceStorageKind.RelationalTables resource, compile a TableWritePlan for every DbTableModel in TablesInDependencyOrder, preserving that order. Each TableWritePlan must include InsertSql, optional UpdateSql, optional DeleteByParentSql, BulkInsertBatching, ColumnBindings, and (temporarily) empty KeyUnificationPlans. Compilation must be deterministic under input-order permutations; failures must throw with actionable diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Update/extend compiler unit tests to include a multi-table resource and assert: one table plan per table in dependency order

description: Compile KeyUnificationWritePlan inventory for tables with key unification classes. Acceptance: for each DbTableModel.KeyUnificationClasses entry, emit exactly one KeyUnificationWritePlan with CanonicalColumn and CanonicalBindingIndex pointing to a WriteValueSource.Precomputed binding for that canonical stored column. Emit MembersInOrder matching KeyUnificationClass.MemberPathColumns order; each member must record MemberPathColumn, scope-relative RelativePath ("$" allowed), and (when present) PresenceColumn and PresenceBindingIndex.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering both ScalarMember and DescriptorMember compilation and ordering invariants

description: Detect and validate synthetic presence columns. Acceptance: PresenceIsSynthetic is true iff PresenceColumn's DbColumnModel.SourceJsonPath is null. When PresenceIsSynthetic is true, validate hard that: (1) the presence column is nullable boolean/bit (RelationalScalarType kind bool + IsNullable true), and (2) the table has a matching TableConstraint.NullOrTrue for that column; otherwise throw. If PresenceIsSynthetic is false, treat it as reference-site presence (e.g., ..._DocumentId) and do not require NullOrTrue.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for: synthetic presence happy path; missing NullOrTrue constraint failure; non-synthetic presence path

description: Enforce key-unification write-plan invariants (fail fast). Acceptance: (1) UnifiedAlias columns are never bound or written: exclude from ColumnBindings and from emitted SQL column lists; any attempt to include them fails compilation. (2) Canonical stored columns and synthetic presence-flag columns must be WriteValueSource.Precomputed. (3) Every WriteValueSource.Precomputed binding must be produced by exactly one KeyUnificationWritePlan; orphan precomputed bindings fail compilation. (4) If a table has any Precomputed bindings, it must have key-unification inventory that accounts for them (treat Precomputed as key-unification-only).
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for: UnifiedAlias write exclusion failure; orphan Precomputed failure; duplicate producer failure; canonical column must be Precomputed

description: Compile write plans for every ResourceStorageKind.RelationalTables resource in MappingSetCompiler. Acceptance: MappingSetCompiler uses the new multi-table write-plan compiler for all relational-table resources (including resources with child tables, _ext tables, and key unification) and populates WritePlansByResource for all of them. Any invariant violation during write-plan compilation throws and fails the whole mapping set. RootOnlyReadPlanCompiler behavior remains unchanged: read plans are compiled only for thin-slice supported resources and omitted otherwise.
steps-to-verify: Update MappingSetCompilerTests to assert: write plan exists for a non-root-only resource; read plan is null/omitted for that resource until story 05

description: Update GetWritePlanOrThrow messaging now that write plans exist for all relational-table resources. Acceptance: after this story, missing write plans for ResourceStorageKind.RelationalTables should be treated as an internal compilation/selection bug (throw InvalidOperationException with mapping set key + resource). SharedDescriptorTable resources should continue to throw NotSupportedException pointing to the descriptor write story. Read-plan lookup behavior and next-story pointers remain unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Update/extend MappingSetLookupExtensionsTests to cover: descriptor write-plan message; relational-table missing-plan treated as bug

description: Generalize mappingset.manifest.json resource inclusion and null plan representation. Acceptance: the manifest emitter includes every resource deterministically and emits both write_plan and read_plan properties as either an object or explicit null (never omit the property). Ordering must be deterministic: mapping sets by key; resources by (project_name, resource_name).
steps-to-verify: Update manifest emitter tests to assert property presence and determinism under input permutation

description: Emit write-plan table inventory in mappingset.manifest.json. Acceptance: for write_plan, emit table_plans_in_dependency_order, each with insert_sql_sha256, update_sql_sha256, delete_by_parent_sql_sha256, bulk_insert_batching, column_bindings_in_order (including write_value_source details), and key_unification_plans. Tables must be in dependency order; bindings must be in binding order; key-unification members must preserve member order (do not sort). SQL hashes must be computed over normalized multiline SQL.
steps-to-verify: Update/extend manifest emitter tests to validate table plan arrays, hashes, and ordering invariants

description: Update the existing minimal thin-slice golden manifest to the generalized schema. Acceptance: Fixtures/runtime-plan-compilation/minimal/expected/mappingset.manifest.json matches the new schema and remains stable across runs for both pgsql and mssql.
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Implement a multi-project plans fixture builder for plan compilation tests. Acceptance: fixture folder contains inputs/ with multiple ApiSchema files plus a tiny fixture manifest listing file names and isExtensionProject flags. Builder loads all inputs, builds an EffectiveSchemaSet with correct IsExtensionProject flags, and compiles DerivedRelationalModelSet for both dialects. Output must be deterministic even when the fixture manifest input order is permuted.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for the multi-project fixture builder (including determinism under input order reversal)

description: Add a collections + nested collections + _ext fixture input set. Acceptance: add a base project resource schema for a small readable resource with addresses[*] and addresses[*].periods[*] plus a few scalars. Add an extension project (IsExtensionProject=true, isResourceExtension=true) that adds _ext.sample fields at: (a) root, (b) inside addresses[*], and (c) inside periods[*]. Include a fixture manifest referencing both inputs.
steps-to-verify: Build the DerivedRelationalModelSet from the fixture for pgsql and mssql and assert extension tables are derived and key-aligned to base scopes

description: Add a golden runtime plan compilation test for the collections + _ext fixture. Acceptance: expected mappingset.manifest.json (pgsql + mssql) includes write plans with correct DeleteByParentSql hashes for child and extension tables, deterministic binding order, batching metadata, and table plans in dependency order. Read_plan must be explicit null for this resource until story 05.
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Add a key-unification presence-gating fixture input set. Acceptance: add a small base project resource schema with two optional descriptor endpoints constrained by an equality constraint such that derivation produces: (a) a canonical stored column with SourceJsonPath null, and (b) synthetic ..._Present columns with SourceJsonPath null and TableConstraint.NullOrTrue constraints. Include a fixture manifest and keep the schema minimal and readable.
steps-to-verify: Build the DerivedRelationalModelSet from the fixture and assert the derived table includes canonical + synthetic presence columns and NullOrTrue constraints

description: Add golden tests for the key-unification presence-gating fixture, plus a negative test. Acceptance: expected mappingset.manifest.json shows Precomputed bindings for canonical + synthetic presence columns, compiled KeyUnificationPlans with PresenceIsSynthetic=true, and stable SQL hashes. Add a deterministic negative test that fails compilation when a synthetic presence column is missing its NullOrTrue constraint (model/derivation bug).
steps-to-verify: Run (to refresh goldens when needed): UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj
